--- Thread (50 messages, 2020-09-15) ---
[2020-09-15 23:01] anesotericman (Esoteric): but that interest rate can be wildly different
[2020-09-15 23:01] anesotericman (Esoteric): it's like 28 point something percent if you need to buy engies and drones
[2020-09-15 23:01] anesotericman (Esoteric): or I forget what for vivids, on the other end
[2020-09-15 23:01] anesotericman (Esoteric): and if you're doing breachproof shadowfangs the right number of interest is in the hundreds
[2020-09-15 23:02] xnor_gate (xnor): How is 28% with engies and drones? I thought if drones didn't require energy, it's still 25%?
[2020-09-15 23:02] xnor_gate (xnor): And so lower w/ needing energy
[2020-09-15 23:02] anesotericman (Esoteric): I mainly like the 33.33% number because of it being more-or-less okay, and producing easy numbers
[2020-09-15 23:02] jamberine: really? I feel like in the situations where it's useful interest shoudl be something like 40%
[2020-09-15 23:02] xnor_gate (xnor): These all seem very high to me. I think at 33% you soak Urban Sentries rather than defending them
[2020-09-15 23:04] anesotericman (Esoteric): ah, so I wasn't saying discounting rate, I was saying interest rate
[2020-09-15 23:04] anesotericman (Esoteric): 33.33% interest = 25% discount
[2020-09-15 23:05] anesotericman (Esoteric): and for 40%...I don't know that it's wrong for sure
[2020-09-15 23:05] anesotericman (Esoteric): but tarsiers seem better than that 40% would indicate
[2020-09-15 23:05] jamberine: I feel like tarsiers are better because of absorb denial
[2020-09-15 23:06] jamberine: where if oyu're assuming that attack has a constant value you shouldn't be considering that
[2020-09-15 23:06] xnor_gate (xnor): Yes, not accounting for absorb really skews the attacker values
[2020-09-15 23:07] xnor_gate (xnor): Scorches are awful without that
[2020-09-15 23:07] anesotericman (Esoteric): well, if interest is 40%, you'd be as happy to buy a 6R attacker as a tarsier. I normally would prefer the tarsier, post absorb
[2020-09-15 23:07] anesotericman (Esoteric): but the comparison isn't unreasonable
[2020-09-15 23:08] xnor_gate (xnor): Lemme translate interest, how does it relate to my f which is 1/discount?
[2020-09-15 23:08] jamberine: I mean that's paying 2 gold for your opponent to have an engi last turn
[2020-09-15 23:08] jamberine: obivously engi is not efficient defense
[2020-09-15 23:08] jamberine: but it seems pretty close
[2020-09-15 23:09] anesotericman (Esoteric): well, it means they can spend that 2 gold on attackers, not just defense though
[2020-09-15 23:10] anesotericman (Esoteric): as I mentioned before, I sort of do the mental math of the increasing cost of late-game defense rather than approximate it with a higher interest rate
[2020-09-15 23:11] anesotericman (Esoteric): but both ways have value, in my mind
[2020-09-15 23:11] xnor_gate (xnor): (The "forever" value of something each turn start now equals 1+1/interest, so interest=0.4 gives forever=3.5.)
[2020-09-15 23:12] jamberine: like you said an attacker is worth like 8.3 or something right at 1.33
[2020-09-15 23:13] jamberine: splitter is like 9% better than that
[2020-09-15 23:13] jamberine: so it seems like you should be using something higher
[2020-09-15 23:13] anesotericman (Esoteric): that just means that splitters are better than drones
[2020-09-15 23:14] xnor_gate (xnor): > like you said an attacker is worth like 8.3 or something right at 1.33
@jamberine what's 1.33 here?
[2020-09-15 23:14] anesotericman (Esoteric): 1 gold now being equivalent to 1.33333333 gold next turn
[2020-09-15 23:14] jamberine: yes, but if everything you're buying is paying you more than drones
[2020-09-15 23:14] anesotericman (Esoteric): AKA 33% interest AKA "drone inflation"
[2020-09-15 23:15] jamberine: then gold now is worth more than 1.33x gold next turn
[2020-09-15 23:15] anesotericman (Esoteric): well, the idea is that if you actually want to get more economy you are looking at that 33% interest (reeaaalll roughly)
[2020-09-15 23:16] xnor_gate (xnor): @jamberine I get a permanent attack is worth 7.97 there, and a splitter is 4% efficient
[2020-09-15 23:16] xnor_gate (xnor): (Of course ignoring absorb)
[2020-09-15 23:16] anesotericman (Esoteric): if you're getting defense, the 33% seems fine too
[2020-09-15 23:16] anesotericman (Esoteric): (wall vs infusion, for instance)
[2020-09-15 23:16] anesotericman (Esoteric): or just droning up for more defense in future
[2020-09-15 23:17] jamberine: maybe I have a different conception of midgame
[2020-09-15 23:18] jamberine: or when we're using this
[2020-09-15 23:18] jamberine: the thing about infusion is I feel like the decision is always relative to the alternative
[2020-09-15 23:18] jamberine: liek if you need defense now you just buy a wall
[2020-09-15 23:19] jamberine: and if you want defense next turn, infusion has to be better than splitter
[2020-09-15 23:19] jamberine: but maybe it's not better than drake
[2020-09-15 23:19] jamberine: like wall and infusion dont' come up against each other I guess
[2020-09-15 23:19] xnor_gate (xnor): Yup, by default wall +4%, infusion +2%, splitter -2%

[2020-09-15 23:20] xnor_gate (xnor): (Default = slightly worse than drone inflation)
[2020-09-15 23:23] anesotericman (Esoteric): I guess the question may become - is redeemer good?
[2020-09-15 23:24] lyra1712 (Lyra): who needs maths for that
[2020-09-15 23:25] silentslayers: Whats a discount rate?
[2020-09-15 23:26] silentslayers: (the 25% thing mentioned)
[2020-09-15 23:27] jamberine: basically how much less is gold worth next turn relative to this turn
[2020-09-15 23:27] silentslayers: I thought thats what the drone inflation number represented
[2020-09-15 23:28] anesotericman (Esoteric): .75 gold last turn = 1 gold this turn = 1.33 gold next turn
[2020-09-15 23:28] anesotericman (Esoteric): just depends what direction in time you're going
[2020-09-15 23:28] jamberine: wait I did get that confused
[2020-09-15 23:28] jamberine: I think I got the formula right
[2020-09-15 23:29] silentslayers: So what's the value of a BT2 gauss charge?
[2020-09-15 23:29] anesotericman (Esoteric): basically, 1 gold now is worth 33% more than a gold next turn but 25% less than a gold last turn
[2020-09-15 23:29] silentslayers: what's the generalized  formula then
[2020-09-15 23:29] silentslayers: for N turns in the future/past
[2020-09-15 23:30] jamberine: just take (1+inflation)^n
[2020-09-15 23:30] jamberine: negative is the past
[2020-09-15 23:32] anesotericman (Esoteric): different formulas depending on exactly what you're asking for, but you can search for "Present Value" formulas if you want to see more stuff
[2020-09-15 23:32] anesotericman (Esoteric): just on google or whatever. This isn't prismata math, it's interest math
[2020-09-15 23:32] silentslayers: what's the 2.333 mean
[2020-09-15 23:33] jamberine: I was using just the percent because I assumed it would be less ambiguous
[2020-09-15 23:33] xnor_gate (xnor): > So what's the value of a BT2 gauss charge?
@SilentSlayers Careful, attack is delayed by half a turn because the opponent builds prompt def on their turn
[2020-09-15 23:33] jamberine: sicne we've been using both
[2020-09-15 23:34] silentslayers: I dunno how to do it so I don't need the careful warning :PepeHands:
[2020-09-15 23:34] anesotericman (Esoteric): I just use Barrier Value and Attack Value...those are the 2.4 and 2.77 numbers I said
[2020-09-15 23:35] anesotericman (Esoteric): so BT2 gauss charge is 2.77 * (1.33)^-2
[2020-09-15 23:35] xnor_gate (xnor): So this is also 2.44 * (1.33)^-2.5 ?
[2020-09-15 23:36] anesotericman (Esoteric): 2.4*(1.33)^(-1.5), yeah
[2020-09-15 23:36] jamberine: but I think you have the right idea
[2020-09-15 23:36] anesotericman (Esoteric): ignoring the rounding stuff
[2020-09-15 23:36] silentslayers: So it's like

(How much it costs them aka. BV) * (part that represents inflation)^(number of turns)?
[2020-09-15 23:36] xnor_gate (xnor): If you look at my sheet, many of the formulas have a power of p
[2020-09-15 23:37] anesotericman (Esoteric): you can use 2.777 * (.75) ^ 2, if that's easier
[2020-09-15 23:37] xnor_gate (xnor): The p is the decay for delay, and the exponent is the number of turns
[2020-09-15 23:38] anesotericman (Esoteric): the (.75)^2 one makes the most intuitive sense to me, I guess
[2020-09-15 23:38] anesotericman (Esoteric): it's saying that anything NEXT turn is only worth 75% of what it'd be worth now
[2020-09-15 23:39] xnor_gate (xnor): There are multiple conventions you can use for attack, depending on what power your absorb into the coeffcient
[2020-09-15 23:39] anesotericman (Esoteric): and since it's two turns out, you have to do that twice
[2020-09-15 23:39] xnor_gate (xnor): My convention is that prompt soak has a 1-turn delay, and next-turn attack has a 1.5-turn delay
[2020-09-15 23:39] anesotericman (Esoteric): the .75 being (1-discounting rate)
[2020-09-15 23:39] anesotericman (Esoteric): equivalent to (1 / interest rate)
[2020-09-15 23:40] silentslayers: So tant ray is like:

(Value of permanent attacker) + (BT2 gauss charge) + (BT3 gauss charge) + (BT4 gauss charge)?
[2020-09-15 23:40] xnor_gate (xnor): `=h*p^1.5*(f + p + p^2 + p^3)`
[2020-09-15 23:41] xnor_gate (xnor): h=immediate health this turn, p=0.75 or whatever for decay, f="forever"=1+p+p^2+...
[2020-09-15 23:41] xnor_gate (xnor): Can also expand the part in parens to `1+2p+2p^2+2p^3+p^4+p^5+p^6+...`
[2020-09-15 23:42] anesotericman (Esoteric): there's a lot of ways to calculate the specifics. probably easiest just to play with numbers until they make sense to you
[2020-09-15 23:42] jamberine: for f you really want to be using 1/1-p though don't you
[2020-09-15 23:42] jamberine: it looks a lot uglier expanding it
[2020-09-15 23:43] xnor_gate (xnor): i agree, but i like the intuition for it
[2020-09-15 23:43] xnor_gate (xnor): but, yes, that's why I use `f` in formulas

--- Thread (50 messages, 2020-09-15 to 2020-09-16) ---
[2020-09-15 23:43] xnor_gate (xnor): For illustration, Iso Kronus is `=2*h*p^2.5/(1-p^2)`, where we also take the geometric sum
[2020-09-15 23:44] xnor_gate (xnor): since `1+p^2+p^4+p^6+...=1/(1-p^2)`
[2020-09-15 23:45] jamberine: about the 40% thing, the reason I chose 40 is because by yujiri's calculations it's close to the recursive inflation for tarsier
[2020-09-15 23:45] jamberine: and I feel like after absorb that seems about right
[2020-09-15 23:45] xnor_gate (xnor): Lemme see what stuff looks like there, with `f=3.5`
[2020-09-15 23:47] anesotericman (Esoteric): my numbers are mainly just so you can 1: Derive everything from saying BV=2.4, Energy = 0, ABCD all 100% efficient, and 2: easy mental math for costs, threat, attackers, etc.
[2020-09-15 23:48] xnor_gate (xnor): @Esoteric You can put in `f=4, bv=2.4` for that
[2020-09-15 23:48] xnor_gate (xnor): I do like how `f=4` makes mental math easy, but it does create some weird consequences
[2020-09-15 23:49] silentslayers: whats the f i didnt understand
[2020-09-15 23:49] xnor_gate (xnor): `f=1/(1-p)`, so from drones `p=0.75` and `f=4`
[2020-09-15 23:49] anesotericman (Esoteric): f is the value of an unclicked drone, in gold
[2020-09-15 23:50] anesotericman (Esoteric): it's something that produces every turn, starting this one
[2020-09-15 23:50] anesotericman (Esoteric): some of these abbreviations are just what xnor is using, btw
[2020-09-15 23:51] xnor_gate (xnor): Yeah, apparently I'm the only one that thinks in terms of `f` rather than inflation
[2020-09-15 23:52] xnor_gate (xnor): Another way of thinking about it is that `f=4` means than `1/4` of value is lost by waiting a turn
[2020-09-15 23:52] jamberine: re: mental math I definitely come from a different perspective then
[2020-09-15 23:52] jamberine: like I dont' think it's useful to do calculations in game
[2020-09-15 23:52] jamberine: because there are so many other factors
[2020-09-15 23:53] xnor_gate (xnor): I like having a few basic things cached, in terms of generic results rather than numbers
[2020-09-15 23:53] jamberine: so if you're looking at it outside you don't need to be able to do mental math
[2020-09-15 23:53] jamberine: well actually I never do the math myself
[2020-09-15 23:53] jamberine: I just look at other people do it
[2020-09-15 23:53] xnor_gate (xnor): For example, math says holding Lancetooth is generally even in wall absorb sets, so from there I think whether this Lancetooth hold is situationally good or not.
[2020-09-15 23:54] xnor_gate (xnor): But math says it's bad for Infusion Grid absorb, so I'd need a stronger situational argument for holding it there
[2020-09-15 23:54] xnor_gate (xnor): (I do the math so you don't have to 🙂 )
[2020-09-15 23:56] jamberine: I remember seeing posts on tooth inflation years ago
[2020-09-15 23:56] jamberine: but I feel like the easier way to look at it is you get +1 absorb and +4 tempo
[2020-09-15 23:56] xnor_gate (xnor): I'd actually say +3 tempo
[2020-09-15 23:56] xnor_gate (xnor): Or you're double-counting the absorb
[2020-09-15 23:57] xnor_gate (xnor): I guess up to you when you count the absorb
[2020-09-15 23:57] xnor_gate (xnor): If it's counted as benefiting one health now, that's 1 of the 4 tempo
[2020-09-15 23:58] jamberine: well the tempo is you not needing to buy that much defense this turn
[2020-09-15 23:58] silentslayers: you get to buy 4 less defense
[2020-09-15 23:58] silentslayers: that's clearly just 4 tempo
[2020-09-15 23:58] xnor_gate (xnor): And next turn you're at 3 health less as a result
[2020-09-15 23:58] xnor_gate (xnor): So you gain 4 health now, lose 3 health next turn, and attack for 2 less
[2020-09-15 23:59] jamberine: what do you mean lose 3 health
[2020-09-16 00:00] xnor_gate (xnor): If you were absorbing on wall, it's gone now
[2020-09-16 00:00] xnor_gate (xnor): In terms of my formulas which apparently only make sense to me, the decision gives `4*h*p-3*h*p^2-2*h*p^1.5` (4 health now, 3 health less next turn, 2 less damage to opp which they benefit from on their next turn)
[2020-09-16 00:01] silentslayers: your logic makes sense if you intend to click lance in the future yeah
[2020-09-16 00:01] anesotericman (Esoteric): you also get 2 threat
[2020-09-16 00:01] anesotericman (Esoteric): depending on how you're counting stuff
[2020-09-16 00:02] xnor_gate (xnor): yes, i'm accounting for that by saying that the click damage wasn't lost now, but for opponent to defend next turn
